mnemonics.title: BFS Memory Tip
mnemonics.content: BFS = Breadth-First = Breadth = Wide. Think of ripples in water expanding outward in all directions from center.

mnemonics.title: DFS Memory Tip
mnemonics.content: DFS = Depth-First = Go deep like diving underwater before coming back up.

mnemonics.title: Optimality Rule
mnemonics.content: BFS finds SHALLOWEST goal → optimal when costs equal. UCS finds CHEAPEST path → optimal with positive costs.

mnemonics.title: Complete Search Remember
mnemonics.content: Complete = C for Complete = Covers all possibilities. BFS and UCS are complete. DFS is Dangerous (not complete without cycle check).

acronyms.usage: Expands shallowest nodes first using queue FIFO order
Explores deepest path first using stack LIFO order
Expands lowest cost path first using priority queue
Repeated DFS with increasing depth limits
Search from both start and goal simultaneously

visualTips.visualization: Imagine pouring water - ripples expand outward in concentric circles, each circle is a depth level
Think of exploring a maze by always taking the rightmost available path until hitting dead end
Picture a priority queue as a funnel where cheapest cost items slide to the bottom and come out first
Imagine peeling an onion - each layer is a depth limit, you go deeper with each iteration

analogies.analogy: BFS is like exploring all rooms on floor 1 before going to floor 2. DFS is like picking one corridor and going to the end before trying another.
Finding a book in library: BFS checks all shelves systematically (thorough but slow). DFS grabs a section and scans thoroughly (faster but might miss better location).
Complete = exhaustive searcher who will eventually check everywhere if time permits. Incomplete = impatient searcher who might give up too early.
Optimal = always finds the best route. Like GPS that guarantees shortest path, not just any path.
